title: setup/edit connection test crashes: call_api 4-tuple unpacked into 3
description: _test_connection in cli.py unpacked runner.call_api() into 3 values (text,_,_) but call_api returns 4 (text, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, logprob_stats). Every 'Test the connection now?' in setup/edit failed with 'ValueError: too many values to unpack (expected 3, got 4)' regardless of whether the endpoint actually worked. Fix: unpack 4 values. Added regression test in test_cli.py.
close_reason: Fixed cli.py:361 to unpack call_api's 4-tuple; added regression test test_test_connection_unpacks_call_api_4tuple (verified it fails without the fix). Live-tested DeepSeek-V4-Flash via makora endpoint: replied OK.

title: E1/E2: distractor probes still lexically separable (subject-name / comparative-grammar) after stock-phrase fix
description:
bench-9bt removed the fixed STOCK PHRASE but the distractor is still separable WITHOUT relevance reasoning:
E1 (arithmetic/state_tracking/redefined_ops/retroactive_edit): the NoOp clause is about a DIFFERENT NAME, and the gold verifier itself skips it via s.startswith(subj+' ') (grading.py:1194). A model that tracks only the queried subject's sentences passes — pure token filter, no relevance.
E2 (ordering): the NoOp is NON-COMPARATIVE ('X wears a red hat') while every real clue contains a comparative+'than'; a regex for 'than' filters all distractors. Code comment concedes 'the grammar is comparison-only'.
FIX (H1/H2): make distractors relevance-true. H1: same-subject/same-container clause about a DIFFERENT ITEM (so subject/container token no longer separates it) and make every numeric verifier ITEM-AWARE. H2: in-grammar comparative on an ORTHOGONAL relation family ('richer than' inside a height ordering) and make _verify_order bind the relation family to the question's superlative so off-axis edges are ignored. Gold must stay a true NoOp and build_dataset(verify=True) must stay green. Update test_distractor_is_noop_without_stock_phrase to assert the new separability resistance.
acceptance_criteria: distractor remains a NoOp (base.gold==dist.gold); verify_gold passes; new distractor is NOT separable by a subject/container/connective token filter; verifiers updated to be item/relation-aware; tests updated and green
notes: Fixed in this commit. H1: arithmetic/state_tracking/retroactive_edit/redefined_ops distractors are now SAME subject/container acting on a DIFFERENT item; _verify_arithmetic and _verify_state made item-aware (retroactive_edit verifier was already item-aware via re.escape(item)). H2: ordering distractor is now an in-grammar comparative on an ORTHOGONAL relation family; _verify_order binds the relation to the question's superlative and ignores off-axis edges. New tests: test_distractor_not_separable_by_token_filter (no new agent token + off-axis), test_naive_solver_now_fails_on_distractor (a relevance-blind solver is now WRONG on distractor items). 128 pass.
close_reason: H1/H2 implemented: relevance-true distractors + item/relation-aware verifiers; contract tests added; full suite green (128).

title: Audit-R4: adversarial red-team — relevance-true distractors, real pivots, premise-flaw, telemetry honesty
description:
Tracking epic for the R4 adversarial audit. Prior passes (R2/R3: bench-9bt, bench-r6u, bench-5zn) fixed the SURFACE of several weaknesses (stock phrase, some chance baselines) but left the STRUCTURE exploitable. R4 findings:
E1/E2 distractor probes are still defeatable by surface token-matching (subject-name filter; comparative-grammar filter) — the gold verifiers themselves demonstrate the shortcut.
E3 retroactive_edit tests deferred evaluation, not backtracking.
E4 integer fallback grader gameable + marker(nums[0]) vs fallback(nums[-1]) inconsistency, fragility reported but never penalized.
E5 unsat_csp sentinel guessable by prior; _split_set carries an overfit guard token.
E6 sequences difficulty is a rule-tier selector, not a depth axis — degradation x-axis non-commensurable across families.
E7 chance baseline assumes 0 for retroactive_edit/multi_turn_inject — verify empirically.
E8 surface invariance holds numeric magnitudes fixed.
Hygiene: duplicate confabulation_rate key, dead 'A nearby' branch, cosmetic multi_turn assistant turn.
Hardening families H1-H8 and a chunk-level TelemetryPayload schema with honest observability provenance.
notes: R4 AUDIT COMPLETE — all hardening waves closed. Final stretch wave: bench-81k (H4 redefined_ops position-dependent + compositional operators), bench-xg3 (H5 unsat_localize + justified_choice answer_type, entailment-graded), bench-32g (H6 composed 5-hop op-changing cascade — early parity slip selects wrong operator downstream). All H1-H8 + telemetry done. Test suite: 143 -> 150 passing; +1 family (unsat_localize); +1 answer_type (justified_choice); redefined_ops and composed restructured. No open children remain.
close_reason: All R4 hardening children closed (H1-H8 + telemetry + final stretch wave H4/H5/H6). 150 tests passing.

title: One-command interactive launcher (start): pick/add model -> generate -> run -> report
description: Add a single entry-point command that runs the whole benchmark interactively. 'python cli.py start' (and bare 'python cli.py') launches a menu: if models are configured, ask to run an existing one (numbered pick) or add a new one (reuse the setup wizard); if none configured, go straight to the wizard. Then orchestrate the full pipeline — reuse the existing dataset or generate one from a size preset (quick/standard/thorough), run the model with the live progress bar, and build the report — with sensible defaults and a single final confirm. Goal: the easiest possible start, one command end to end.
close_reason: Added 'start' command (and bare 'python cli.py') — interactive launcher: pick an existing model by number or add one via the setup wizard, reuse-or-generate a dataset (quick/standard/thorough preset), then run with the live progress bar and build the report. Refactored setup into reusable _setup_wizard; EOF/Ctrl-C now abort cleanly. 4 new tests; 120 pass.

title: cmd_run crashes in --mock mode (UnboundLocalError: ep)
description: cmd_run references ep.get('capabilities') unconditionally, but ep is only assigned in the non-mock branch. Any 'cli.py run --mock ...' (including the README quickstart) raises UnboundLocalError before the run starts. Tests call runner.run directly so they missed it. Introduced with the native-anthropic capabilities plumbing.
close_reason: Fixed UnboundLocalError: cmd_run referenced undefined 'ep' in --mock mode. Initialized capabilities=[] in the mock branch. Added regression test test_cmd_run_mock_does_not_crash.

title: Onboarding: setup wizard + live run progress + easy-start docs
description: Make the benchmark trivial to start for newcomers. Three parts: (1) an interactive 'setup' CLI wizard that prompts step-by-step for provider endpoint, API key, model id, and context window, writes them to providers.json, and prints next steps; (2) a live progress display during 'run' (progress bar, counts, rate, ETA, errors) so the benchmark shows visible progress instead of an occasional N/total line; (3) a 'super easy' getting-started section in the READMEs pointing at the wizard.
close_reason: Added 'setup' interactive wizard (endpoint/key/model-id/context-window -> providers.json + next steps), live run progress bar (percent/counts/rate/ETA, non-TTY fallback + done summary), and easy-start docs in both READMEs. 11 new tests; 116 pass.

title: chance baselines wrong/skewed in metrics (composed int, unsat_csp + logic_grid skew)
description: metrics._chance_baseline (metrics.py:52) returns 1/(difficulty+2) for composed, but composed now returns an UNBOUNDED INT (gold=str(arith_result)) -> chance should be ~0; this understates composed acc_above_chance. unsat_csp uses a flat 0.25 but the realized label prior is ~0.28 majority-class; logic_grid uses 1/n but floors are skewed low -> both overstate acc_above_chance. Fix: int families -> 0.0; choice/set families -> empirical modal-answer frequency from realized golds (honest best-constant-guess baseline). Update test_metrics.py assertions (they encode the stale 'composed=1/5' contract).
close_reason: Replaced theoretical _chance_baseline with empirical best-constant-guess (_modal_frequency). Unbounded-int families (incl. composed) -> 0; bounded families (ordering/kk/unsat_csp/logic_grid) -> realized modal-answer frequency. Fixes composed (0.22->0), unsat_csp flat-0.25-overstatement (->0.32 empirical), kk 1/2^n understatement (->0.08), and keeps logic_grid empirical despite int answer_type. Tests updated.

title: composed family collapses to one hop (leaked count + decorative stages)
description: Red-team audit (Audit-R3). gen_composed (generators.py:697) claims >=3 execution-dependency hops but: (1) Stage 1's knight count is printed verbatim in Stage 2 as 'starts with N' (:719), so hop A never needs solving; (2) Stage 3 is labelled '(distractor, not used for the answer)' (:766) and the prompt says 'Stage 2's result is the answer' (:767). Net: collapses to a single arithmetic chain on a plaintext number. Fix: do NOT print the knight count (reference it verbally); make Stage 3 load-bearing (final gold = R * C where C is the re-derived ordering position); update _composed_parse_hops/_verify_composed/_verify_arithmetic_raw. Acceptance: gold verifies; perturbing hop A OR hop C changes the gold; no stage labelled distractor.
close_reason: Rebuilt gen_composed: knight count is deduced (never printed - 'as many as there are knights'), Stage 3 ordering is load-bearing (gold = R*C where C is the re-derived pivot position), all 3 hops independently re-verified. No 'distractor' label, no modulo masking. New test test_composed_hop_c_is_load_bearing; updated perturb test. 97 tests pass.

title: New reasoning families: planning (blocksworld) + theory-of-mind
description: Procedurally-generated planning (blocksworld/river-crossing; search depth = difficulty) and theory-of-mind (nested beliefs) generators with verify_gold support, registered in GENERATORS. Contamination-free, difficulty-scaled.
close_reason: Fanned out across Haiku/Opus/Sonnet. #1 intelligence metrics (reasoning_by_difficulty, correct_per_1k efficiency, effort_scaling) in runtime_stats + dashboard + runreport. #2 planning_blocksworld + theory_of_mind families with BFS/belief verifiers. #3 code_output (SWE-lite) family with self-exec gold + verifier. 216 tests pass; e2e over all 3 families green.

title: Intelligence metrics: efficiency frontier + overthinking profile
description: Post-hoc reasoning-effort metrics from telemetry: corrects per 1k reasoning tokens (efficiency), reasoning tokens & accuracy by difficulty (overthinking/effort-scaling signal). Surface in runtime_stats -> dashboard + runreport. Builds on reasoning-token capture (bench-3hi).
close_reason: Fanned out across Haiku/Opus/Sonnet. #1 intelligence metrics (reasoning_by_difficulty, correct_per_1k efficiency, effort_scaling) in runtime_stats + dashboard + runreport. #2 planning_blocksworld + theory_of_mind families with BFS/belief verifiers. #3 code_output (SWE-lite) family with self-exec gold + verifier. 216 tests pass; e2e over all 3 families green.

title: Capture + report reasoning tokens (counts, density, efficiency)
description: OpenAI-compatible call_api discards usage.completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens; capture it and thread into telemetry. Aggregate in runtime_stats (total, mean, fraction of completion, tokens-per-correct) and surface in dashboard + runreport. No proxy needed — read provider usage in-process.
close_reason: Implemented: run-time --min-diff/--max-diff/--limit filtering + interactive launch prompts; reasoning-token capture from usage.completion_tokens_details + Anthropic native, aggregated (total/mean/fraction/per-correct) in runtime_stats and surfaced in dashboard + runreport. 202 tests pass.

title: Run-time difficulty/amount filtering for launches
description: Add --min-diff/--max-diff/--limit to the run command and interactive launch prompts so a single generated pool can be run in slices (e.g. hard-only, N items) without regenerating. Limit takes an even stride across the filtered set so families/difficulties stay represented.
close_reason: Implemented: run-time --min-diff/--max-diff/--limit filtering + interactive launch prompts; reasoning-token capture from usage.completion_tokens_details + Anthropic native, aggregated (total/mean/fraction/per-correct) in runtime_stats and surfaced in dashboard + runreport. 202 tests pass.

title: Interactive start: let user pick difficulty band + amount of tests
description: The interactive launcher (cli.py start) only offers fixed size presets via _ensure_dataset, so there's no way to generate e.g. only hard problems and skip easy ones. Add a difficulty-band selector (all/easy/medium/hard, default = preset range) and an amount (reps) prompt to the start flow. Non-interactive generate already supports --min-diff/--max-diff/--reps.
close_reason: Added difficulty-band + amount selection to interactive start flow; non-interactive generate already supported these flags. Tests added/updated, all passing.

title: Per-run report folder: detailed solved/unsolved breakdown + HTML
description: After every benchmark run, write a per-run output folder (named by model+run-id+date) containing: a detailed report (which items solved, which not, and WHY), a plain-text run log, the full results JSON, and an HTML file named with model+date+results. Wire into cmd_run so it happens automatically after each run.
close_reason: Added runreport.py: per-run folder with detailed solved/unsolved-and-why report, model+date HTML, run.log, items.csv, results JSON; wired into cmd_run; tests in test_runreport.py + updated test_cli.py. 196 tests pass.

title: Export per-run results to results/<model>_<run>_<date>.json after each run
description: After a run finishes, cmd_run should write a complete record (run metadata with api key redacted, full metrics + runtime stats, every graded per-item response) to a results/ folder, filename = model + run-id + date. Add --results-dir (default results/). Wire into cmd_run so the start flow gets it too.
close_reason: Added _export_results + --results-dir (default results/). cmd_run writes results/<model>_<run-id>_<date>.json after every finished run: run metadata (api key redacted), full metrics + runtime stats, every per-item response incl. raw output. Wired so the start flow exports too. 3 tests added, 39 pass, verified end-to-end.

title: Connection test fails for reasoning models (max_tokens=16 too small)
description: setup/edit connection test sends max_tokens=16. Reasoning models (Kimi-K2.7, DeepSeek-R1/V4, o1-style) spend completion tokens on reasoning before emitting content, so they hit finish_reason=length with content=null and the test raises 'response choice missing message.content'. The endpoint/key/model are actually fine. Fix: give the test a real token budget, and make call_api's error name the truncation cause.
close_reason: Bumped connection-test budget 16->512 (reasoning models burn completion tokens before content); call_api now names the token-cap truncation cause. Verified live against Kimi-K2.7 endpoint + 37 tests pass.

title: Adopt uv (pyproject.toml + uv.lock) as the package manager, replacing pip/requirements.txt
description: Project requested switch from pip to uv. Add files/pyproject.toml (numpy+matplotlib deps, pytest dev group, tool.uv.package=false for the flat-script layout) and a committed uv.lock; remove files/requirements.txt; update README.md, files/README.md, GETTING_STARTED.md, and the cli.py matplotlib hint to the uv sync / uv run workflow.
close_reason: Done: files/pyproject.toml + committed uv.lock added (numpy, matplotlib, pytest dev group, package=false); requirements.txt removed; all docs + cli.py hint switched to uv sync / uv run. Verified uv sync, uv run python cli.py (full smoke flow), and uv run pytest -q (169 passed).

title: Terminal dashboard: rich in-terminal stats display for benchmark runs
description: The pipeline has a live progress bar, a plain-text metrics.print_summary, and matplotlib PNG charts (report.py). There is no rich in-terminal dashboard — to see results visually you must open PNGs. Build a dependency-free (stdlib-only, matching runner's philosophy) terminal dashboard that renders metrics as colored bars, inline degradation sparklines, calibration, distractibility, and runtime/token stats. TTY/NO_COLOR/unicode-aware with graceful ASCII+plain fallbacks (like _Progress). Add a 'dashboard' CLI subcommand (single run + multi-run compare), surface new runtime stats (latency percentiles, token totals) from the responses table, wire into 'start', and add tests driven by the deterministic mock.
close_reason: Added dependency-free terminal dashboard (dashboard.py): colored meters, per-family degradation sparklines, calibration diagram, distractibility, runtime/token stats, and a multi-run leaderboard. TTY/NO_COLOR/unicode-aware with ASCII+plain fallbacks. New 'dashboard' CLI subcommand, runtime_stats in metrics, wired into 'start' as finale, 19 new tests (all 169 pass), README+manual updated.

title: H6: 5-hop composed cascade where an early error changes a later OPERATION
description: Extend composed to >=4 non-linear hops where a slip in an early hop changes the OPERATION selected in a later hop (e.g. parity of hop B picks the operator in hop D), not just the magnitude — so stochastic guessing collapses. Each hop independently verifiable.
close_reason: composed is now a 5-hop cascade (knights->arith->ordering->two parity-gated op-selections); an early slip flips a parity and selects a different OPERATOR downstream. Gate operators randomized+read from text; verifier re-derives all 5 hops; new load-bearing test added

title: H5: unsat_csp justification-localization (justified_choice answer_type)
description: From E5/bench-2am: an unsat_csp variant that requires LOCALIZING which clue to reject (or which pair contradicts), graded by an entailment/justification check, so sentinel-guessing-by-prior no longer suffices. Needs a new 'justified_choice' answer_type in grading + metrics.
close_reason: new unsat_localize family + justified_choice answer_type: localize which statement to remove to restore satisfiability; graded by an entailment/justification check (verify re-derives the unique restorer); flaw position randomized so the unsat_csp sentinel-prior no longer wins. Wired through grading, metrics, runner; 3 tests added

title: H4: redefined_ops with position-dependent + compositional operators
description: Strengthen redefined_ops (or a new family) so operators depend on POSITION in the chain (e.g. first two uses mean a+b, then a-b) and one operator is defined IN TERMS OF another (compositional). Defeats the i%2 positional shortcut and forces independent per-step application. Verifier replays the position-aware/compositional semantics from prompt text.
close_reason: redefined_ops now has position-dependent and compositional operator modes at difficulty>=3; verifier replays both independently; 2 load-bearing tests added

title: H4/H6/H7/H8: next-gen adversarial families (counterfactual cost algebra, 5-hop cascade, false_lemma, noise_haystack)
description: H4 redefined_ops with position-dependent + compositionally-defined operators (defeats i%2 shortcut). H6 5-hop composed cascade where an error in an early hop changes the OPERATION (not just the number) in a later hop. H7 false_lemma: a plausible-but-wrong 'useful fact' the model must reject or fail deterministically. H8 noise_haystack: wrap a base item in 600-1000 tokens of embedding-adjacent correct-but-irrelevant filler. All keep three-RNG + construct-then-independently-reverify discipline.
notes: Also absorb H5 (from E5/bench-2am): an unsat_csp variant that requires LOCALIZING which clue to reject with a graded justification (new 'justified_choice' answer_type), so sentinel-guessing-by-prior no longer suffices.
close_reason: Delivered H7 (false_lemma — premise-flaw trap) and H8 (noise_haystack — high-similarity needle), both fully procedural + independently verified, with naive-solver-fooled tests. Remaining vectors split into focused child issues: bench-81k (H4 position/compositional ops), bench-xg3 (H5 justification-localization), bench-32g (H6 5-hop op-changing cascade).

title: E5: unsat_csp sentinel guessable by prior; _split_set carries overfit guard token
description: unsat_csp labels are engineered ~uniform over {knight,knave,UNDETERMINED,NO_SOLUTION} (generators.py:997), so a model with a learned 'trick puzzle' prior farms the confabulation metric by guessing sentinels at base rate. Separately _split_set hard-codes a guard token 'knight,knave,knight' (grading.py:145) — a scorer overfit to one model's output quirk; remove/generalize it. H5: require localizing WHICH clue to reject with a graded justification (new justified_choice answer_type).
close_reason: Removed the dead+overfit '_split_set' guard token 'knight,knave,knight' and simplified the function. Surfaced+fixed a real bug: 'ANSWER: none' on an all-knight puzzle (gold '') now grades correct — a present marker is authoritative for set answers instead of falling through to a full-text scan. Prior-guessing on unsat_csp is already neutralized by chance-correction (bench-r6u) + false_undetermined_rate; the deeper justification-localization (H5) is moved to bench-dqw. +1 test (132 pass).

title: E6: sequences difficulty is a rule-tier selector, not a depth axis — degradation x-axis non-commensurable
description: gen_sequence maps difficulty->rule type (d1=AP..d6=cubic, generators.py:374). The README sells degradation as 'accuracy as you add reasoning steps' but for sequences (and n-entity families) the x-axis means different things, so cross-family degradation comparison is invalid. Fix: either relabel the sequences axis as 'rule tier' in report/README, or separate depth-scaled vs tier-scaled families in the degradation plot so they are not implicitly compared.
close_reason: Added generators.DIFFICULTY_AXIS + difficulty_axis(): the per-family meaning of 'difficulty' (reasoning steps vs sequences rule-tier vs CSP problem-size n). Report degradation chart now labels each subplot's x-axis honestly and the suptitle states axes are not commensurable; print_summary annotates each family's axis; both READMEs corrected. Also fixed a latent write_csv crash (pass@k_oracle assumed passk non-None for n=1 runs). +1 test, 133 pass.

title: E3/H3: retroactive_edit tests deferred eval, not backtracking — add commit-before-pivot variant
description: gen_retroactive_edit only rescales an INITIAL value and recomputes by replaying from initial state (generators.py:227-243). A model that reads all clauses then computes never commits a pre-edit path, so nothing is invalidated mid-stream — zero backtracking. H3: ask for an intermediate answer at step k, commit/grade it, THEN inject a clause that retroactively reinterprets earlier ops; gold requires re-deriving the committed intermediate. Needs Problem.subgolds + per-hop verification.
close_reason: Added dynamic_pivot family: genuine commit-then-backtrack. Turn 1 commits a literal count (subgold) WITHOUT seeing the pivot; turn 2 reveals 'moved' ops never happened, forcing a revision (gold). Runner now does genuine sequential multi-turn for items carrying a subgold (the model answers each turn before the next is shown) — every other path untouched. metrics exposes a backtracking section (commit accuracy, revised accuracy, revision-success|correct-commit); surfaced in print_summary + report. Problem/storage carry subgold; response metadata carries the intermediate grade. Mock path fully tested end-to-end (perfect->all 1.0; noisy->revision-success 0.59); the real-API per-turn calls reuse the existing call_anthropic/call_api in a simple loop (not exercised against a live model here). Also removed a dead duplicate _mk. +2 tests, 137 pass.

title: E4: integer fallback grader gameable; marker(nums[0]) vs fallback(nums[-1]) inconsistent; fragility never penalized
description: parse_answer marker path takes nums[0] of the marker (grading.py:61); fallback takes nums[-1] of the whole stripped text (grading.py:91-93). For arithmetic the running total is often the last integer, so a model that never emits ANSWER: still scores. grading_fragility measures the marker/fallback disagreement (metrics.py:343) but it is reported, never deducted. Decide: make fallback consistent, and/or surface a fragility-discounted accuracy.
close_reason: Documented the deliberate marker(first-int)/fallback(last-int) asymmetry; added strict (marker-only) accuracy + fallback_reliance to metrics, surfaced in print_summary/report.md/CSV. Fragility now has teeth. +3 tests, 131 pass.

title: dynamic-constraint pivots sometimes no-op; unsat_csp/redefined_ops prior+template imbalance
description: (a) retroactive_edit: queried container qi (:206) is independent of edited container edit_i (:194); when they differ and no move links them, the 'Actually...' pivot does not change the answer -> model can ignore it and still be right. Force qi==edit_i or make a downstream op depend on the edit. multi_turn_inject's injected rule is a visible 3-way multiplier in plaintext (:279). (b) unsat_csp label prior skewed (UNDETERMINED only ~17%); rebalance branch probabilities (:946-997) toward near-uniform labels. (c) redefined_ops: 'add then double' has no bias param so appears ~430x vs ~100x for others (:797) -> give every template a bias param or rotate evenly. MIGRATION.
close_reason: (a) retroactive_edit: query the EDITED container (qi=edit_i) so the retroactive factor is ALWAYS load-bearing (coefficient-1 dependence on scaled initial) -- verified 0/900 non-load-bearing (was no-op ~2/3 of the time). (b) unsat_csp: rebalanced branch probabilities + query a VARYING slot on the dropped-clue class -> label prior knight 26%/knave 26%/UNDETERMINED 24%/NO_SOLUTION 23% (UNDETERMINED was 17%); kept ~20% invariant-slot queries to preserve the le7.1/le7.2 determinate-despite-ambiguity probe (rewrote those tests to scan seeds instead of hardcoding). (c) redefined_ops: gave the bias-less 'double' template a bias param -> balanced (was ~430x over-represented). multi_turn_inject left as-is: its injected rule is always applied (not a no-op) and the unbounded base count makes the final answer non-guessable despite the 3-way multiplier. New tests: label-prior balance, retroactive load-bearing. 105 pass. NOTE: (a)/(b)/(c) change prompts+golds under the same item_id -> dataset-version bump needed before comparing real-model runs pre/post.

title: distractor (NoOp) clauses are lexically separable by a fixed stock phrase
description: Every --distractor clause is a stock phrase + an entity used exactly once: arithmetic '...in a basket' (:110), state_tracking/retroactive 'A nearby ... also holds' (:150,:210), ordering '... wearing a ... hat' (:339), redefined_ops '... also has N pencils' (:846). Worse, the gold verifiers themselves skip the distractor by matching these phrases (e.g. _verify_state skips 'A nearby', :1141), so a model can replicate the verifier heuristic and pass the distractibility probe without relevance reasoning. The probe measures phrase-spotting, not relevance. Fix: sample distractor phrasing + entities from the same template/name/item pool as real clauses. MIGRATION (changes prompts under same item_id) -> dataset-version bump.
close_reason: Hardened all 5 distractor families (arithmetic/state_tracking/ordering/retroactive_edit/redefined_ops): NoOp clauses now match the surface form of real clauses (same item, different subject/container; ordering rotates non-comparison templates). Removed verifier 'A nearby' stock-phrase skips -- verifiers now skip structurally (by subject/container). Verified NoOp preserved + golds verify across 3600 items. New parametrized test test_distractor_is_noop_without_stock_phrase. 103 pass. NOTE: ordering distractor remains non-comparative (its clue grammar is comparison-only) but no longer a single stock phrase.

title: Code-reasoning family (SWE-lite): sandboxed code-output prediction
description: Generate small deterministic functions; model predicts the return value; grade by executing in a restricted subprocess sandbox. In-architecture stepping stone toward code benchmarks; no repos/Docker. Needs a safe executor.
close_reason: Fanned out across Haiku/Opus/Sonnet. #1 intelligence metrics (reasoning_by_difficulty, correct_per_1k efficiency, effort_scaling) in runtime_stats + dashboard + runreport. #2 planning_blocksworld + theory_of_mind families with BFS/belief verifiers. #3 code_output (SWE-lite) family with self-exec gold + verifier. 216 tests pass; e2e over all 3 families green.

title: H-telemetry: chunk-level TelemetryPayload schema + open-weights logprob provider path
description: Promote runner.py _build_telemetry's unobservable_fields into a first-class per-metric observability contract; emit a chunk-level stream array (phase boundaries on hosted Anthropic, per-token entropy on a logprob path). Add a vllm/tgi provider with a 'logprobs' capability and _parse_logprob_stream that fills entropy_bits/branch_event/tot_graph; those fields stay null+annotated on the Anthropic display:omitted path. Schema drafted in the R4 audit.
close_reason: Delivered the observable, testable core of the telemetry mandate: token-entropy from logprobs on OpenAI-compatible/open-weights endpoints. token_entropy_stats() computes per-token H(X) over the renormalized top-k distribution + friction_transitions + logprob_divergence_spikes; call_api requests logprobs when a 'logprobs' capability is set; _build_telemetry populates entropy fields when present and annotates them unobservable otherwise; storage gains the columns. Honest observability contract kept: ToT branch map / thinking-tps stay null+annotated (physically unobservable without raw CoT). Added telemetry_schema.json (matches emitted payload). Fixed a latent missing 'import math' in runner. +2 tests, 143 pass. The full speculative chunk-level streaming/ToT schema remains documented in the Phase-3 design but unbuilt (unobservable on hosted CoT).

title: E-hygiene: duplicate confabulation_rate key, dead 'A nearby' branch, cosmetic multi_turn assistant turn
description: metrics.py emits confabulation_rate twice in the result dict (lines ~391 and ~395). grading.py _verify_multi_turn_inject has a dead 'if s.startswith("A nearby")' branch (line ~1441) referencing a distractor style no longer generated. runner.py:78 injects a canned assistant turn 'Understood. Current state noted.' that carries no state, making multi_turn_inject effectively single-context. Clean up under the Boy Scout rule.
close_reason: Removed duplicate confabulation_rate key in metrics return dict; removed dead 'A nearby' branch in _verify_multi_turn_inject (multi_turn_inject emits no such distractor); replaced misleading 'Current state noted.' assistant turn with a neutral acknowledgement + comment explaining the probe must re-read turn-1 facts across the boundary. 131 pass.

title: E8: surface invariance holds numeric magnitudes fixed — under-tests robustness
description: Surface variants change only names/items from fixed pools (generators.py:31); numeric magnitudes are held fixed (only surface rng moves). So 'surface invariance' proves robustness to these 20 names, not to magnitude/framing. Add a magnitude-perturbing surface variant that re-rolls numbers while preserving the computation structure and gold-by-construction, OR document the scope limit in README.
close_reason: Moved cosmetic verb choice in gen_arithmetic from the structure rng to the SURFACE rng, so surface variants now vary lexical phrasing (not just names/items) while the gold stays fixed across the grp — the invariance probe tests lexical robustness too. Documented the inherent scope limit in both READMEs: numeric magnitude cannot vary within a same-gold flip pair (it changes the gold), so magnitude robustness is read from degradation/variance, not the flip metric. +1 test, 134 pass.

title: E7: chance baseline assumes 0 for retroactive_edit/multi_turn_inject — verify empirically
description: _UNBOUNDED_INT_FAMILIES (metrics.py:70) hard-codes chance~0 for retroactive_edit and multi_turn_inject, inflating acc_above_chance=(acc-chance)/(1-chance). Their golds are small-range counts; the x2-x5 / x1-x3 scaling MAY spread them enough to justify ~0, but this is assumed not measured. Action: empirically compute realized modal-answer frequency for these families over a large build; if non-trivial (>~0.05) switch them to the empirical _modal_frequency path (already used for bounded families). Lower priority — may be a no-op after measurement.
close_reason: Measured realized modal-gold frequency over 1200 items/family: retroactive_edit=0.026, multi_turn_inject=0.049 — both BELOW arithmetic(0.070)/state_tracking(0.052) which are already in the unbounded set. The x2-5 scaling spreads the answer distribution, so chance~0 is correct. No change needed.

title: sequence verifier hollow ambiguity gate + dead code / hygiene
description: _verify_sequence's ambiguity check (generators.py:1261-1266) is a no-op ('pass # accept the higher-degree fit'), so the README's 'unique next term' guarantee is not enforced -- add a real finite-difference ambiguity gate that flags genuinely ambiguous sequences. Also: _detect_next (:1209-1235) is dead code (no caller) -> remove. runner.py:365 has an unreachable second return -> remove. README says 'Thirteen problem families' but lists 11 -> fix count.
close_reason: Real ambiguity gate in _verify_sequence: collects next-term predictions from all fitting rules (poly via finite-diff with >=3 confirming differences, geometric, fibonacci, interleaved-AP) and rejects when >1 distinct prediction. Verified: 0 false-flags across 3600 generator sequences; flags genuinely ambiguous [3,6,12,24]/[2,4,8,16]. Deleted dead _detect_next; removed unreachable runner.py return; README 'Thirteen'->'Eleven'. New test test_sequence_verifier_flags_ambiguous. 98 pass.

title: EPIC: Real SWE-bench agentic harness
description: Separate subsystem: clone repo at commit, give model an agentic edit loop, apply patch, run real test suite in Docker, grade pass/fail. Large + contamination-prone; needs its own design doc before implementation. Tracked so it isn't lost; not started.
